candidates.name: Take telegraph messenger job over higher-paying mill work for exposure to businessmen
First $500 investment in Adams Express via Scott loan against mother's house
Unilaterally restart blocked freight at PRR during Scott's absence
Accept Civil War mobilization assignment from Scott despite cost to private investments
Negotiate harder Allegheny Valley Railroad bond terms despite commission reduction
Visit Bessemer's Sheffield works in 1872 and commit immediately to Edgar Thomson
Name Edgar Thomson Works after PRR president to lock in customer relationship
Push Edgar Thomson construction through 1873 Panic
Hire Captain Bill Jones at industry-record salary with operational autonomy
Commit Edgar Thomson to high-volume rail production over higher-margin specialty steel
Found Keystone Bridge as complementary-competence partnership with engineers
Pursue the Eads Bridge contract for reputational return even at unit-economic cost
Backward integrate into Kloman iron mill to control quality for Eads Bridge
Retain Keystone engineering capacity through 1873-79 depression
Acquire 11.25% of Frick Coke in 1881 to internalize coking input cost
Elevate Frick to chairman of Carnegie Brothers in 1889
Acquire Homestead Works via stock-swap from distressed consortium
Pre-authorize hard line on Amalgamated at Homestead while remaining in Scotland
Send 'we approve of anything you do' telegram to Frick during Pinkerton confrontation
Refuse to publicly defend Frick or share moral cost after Homestead
Accelerate library philanthropy starting 1892 as Homestead counter-narrative
Underbid competitors aggressively during 1893-97 depression to drive consolidation
Sign 50-year Mesabi Range ore lease with Rockefeller in 1896
Build the Bessemer & Lake Erie Railroad to control ore transport
Attempt to force Frick out via Iron Clad at book value in 1899
Commission visible tube-mill expansion plans in 1900 to threaten Morgan downstream
Write $480M price on a single sheet of paper via Schwab
Take consideration in U.S. Steel bonds rather than cash
Publish Gospel of Wealth in 1889 as commitment device
Require matching commitment from communities receiving libraries
Refuse to fund operating endowments for libraries (capital grant only)
Standardize library architectural plans (Bertram's notes) for scaling
Publish Triumphant Democracy in 1886 as macro investment thesis
Continue personal correspondence with Kaiser Wilhelm II 1907-1914 on peace
Establish Carnegie Endowment for International Peace in 1910 with mission-completion provision
